record.field: YOURLS is the self-hosted, de facto standard URL shortener that gives you full ownership of your short links and their data.

YOURLS is the self-hosted, de facto standard URL shortener that gives you full ownership of your short links and their data.

Written in PHP, YOURLS lets you run your own link-shortening service (an alternative to TinyURL or Bitly) on your own server and domain. It offers per-link statistics, a clean admin panel, and a documented API, and it is extended through a mature plugin ecosystem. It suits anyone who wants branded short links without handing analytics and click data to a third party.

Key features
  • Custom and automatic short URLs on your own domain
  • Detailed per-link click statistics (geolocation, referrers, history)
  • Web admin panel plus a programmatic HTTP API
  • Public or private mode for personal or shared use
  • Extensible plugin and filter/action hook system
  • Bookmarklets and bulk operations for fast link creation
Best for

Individuals, businesses, and teams who want branded short links with private, self-owned analytics, and developers who need a scriptable shortener behind their own infrastructure.

services: yourls-app: image: yourls:1.9.2 environment: YOURLS_SITE: https://{{ yourls_host }} YOURLS_USER: {{ yourls_admin_user }} YOURLS_PASS: {{ yourls_admin_password }} YOURLS_DB_HOST: yourls-mysql YOURLS_DB_USER: yourls YOURLS_DB_PASS: {{ mysql_password }} YOURLS_DB_NAME: yourls volumes: - ./yourls_data:/var/www/html depends_on: yourls-mysql: condition: service_healthy restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network yourls-mysql: image: mysql:5.7 environment: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: {{ mysql_root_password }} MYSQL_DATABASE: yourls MYSQL_USER: yourls MYSQL_PASSWORD: {{ mysql_password }} volumes: - ./yourls_mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql healthcheck: test: - CMD - mysqladmin - ping - -h - localhost - -u$$MYSQL_USER - -p$$MYSQL_PASSWORD interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_yourls_mysql' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T yourls-mysql sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d yourls-mysql # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T yourls-mysql sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
